of.d: belonging to or related to something
of.e: The book is full of stories.

on.d: a word that shows something is above or touching something else
on.e: The book is sitting on the table.

or.e: Do you want juice or milk?

out.d: away from the inside of a place
out.e: The cat ran out of the house.

other.d: Other means something or someone different from the one mentioned.
other.e: I have a red ball and a blue one. The other one is green.

only.d: Only means just one and no more.
only.e: I have only one cookie left.

our.d: Our shows that something belongs to a group of people.

over.e: The bridge goes over the river.

off.e: I turned the lamp off when I left the room.

old.d: Having existed for a long time
old.e: My grandpa is very old and wise.

own.e: I have my own special toy to play with.

once.e: I went to the park once last week.

order.d: The way things are arranged or put together
order.e: Please put the books in order on the shelf.

oh.d: A word used to show surprise
oh.e: Oh, I didn't know you were coming over today.

open.e: The door to the classroom was open.

often.e: I often go to the library on Saturdays.

others.e: The others in my class are also good students.

office.d: a room or building where people work
office.e: My dad works in a big office downtown.

outside.d: In the area around a building or place
outside.e: I like to play outside in the nice weather.

official.d: Someone who has an important job or role
official.e: The mayor is the official in charge of the city.

online.d: Using the internet to do things
online.e: I play games online with my friends.

original.d: The first or very earliest version of something.
original.e: The artist painted an original design for the new mural.

ok.d: Saying that something is good or alright
ok.e: The teacher said my work was ok.

okay.d: When something is good or acceptable
okay.e: The weather is okay for playing outside.

october.e: My birthday is in October, the fall season.

oil.d: A thick, slippery liquid used for cooking or in machines
oil.e: I need to add more oil to my car's engine.

ones.d: The special or important people or things.
ones.e: My family is the most important ones in my life.

offer.d: To give someone something or ask them to do something
offer.e: The store offered a special deal on toys this week.

officer.d: A person who helps keep people safe
officer.e: The police officer directed traffic after the accident

operation.d: a set of actions done to achieve a goal
operation.e: The operation to fix the broken toy was successful.

organization.d: A group of people working together for a common goal
organization.e: The school has a great organization that plans fun activities.

opening.d: A space or hole that something can go through or into.
opening.e: The new store has a grand opening this weekend.

opinion.d: What you think or believe about something.
opinion.e: In my opinion, the new book is very interesting to read.

opportunity.d: A chance to do something or make something happen.
opportunity.e: The summer camp gave me the opportunity to try new activities.

older.d: more advanced in age than someone or something else
older.e: My older sister is in high school.

operations.d: the work or actions needed to do something
operations.e: The hospital needs to plan its operations carefully.

officers.d: People who help keep order and follow the law.
officers.e: The police officers came to help with the accident.

offered.e: The store offered a special deal today.

otherwise.e: I would have gone to the park, otherwise I stayed home.

opened.d: To cause something to no longer be closed or shut
opened.e: I opened the door to let the dog outside.

obviously.d: Clearly or in a way that is easy to see or understand
obviously.e: She obviously enjoyed the movie, she was laughing the whole time.

orders.d: Instructions telling someone what to do.
orders.e: The chef gave the cooks new orders for making the meals.

offers.d: To provide or give something to someone.
offers.e: The store offers a special deal on books this weekend.

owner.d: The person who owns or controls something.
owner.e: The owner of the pet store takes good care of all the animals.

onto.d: to or in the top of something
onto.e: The cat jumped onto the table.

operating.d: to make something work or run

ordered.d: to put things in a particular arrangement or sequence
ordered.e: The teacher ordered the students to line up before they went to the cafeteria.

options.e: I have several options for my birthday party.

organizations.d: Groups of people who work together for a common goal
organizations.e: The soccer club is a great organization.

officials.d: People who have an important job or role in a group or organization.
officials.e: The school officials decided to have a field day.

originally.d: At the very first time or in the beginning.
originally.e: The toy was originally made in this country.

owned.d: To have something that belongs to you.
owned.e: The family owned a big farm with many animals.

opposition.e: The team put up a strong opposition during the game.

ocean.d: A very big body of water that covers much of the Earth
ocean.e: The ocean is so big that you can't see the other side.

obama.d: Obama is the name of a former president of the United States.
obama.e: Barack Obama was the 44th president of the United States.

origin.e: The origin of the family tradition is from long ago.

obvious.e: It was obvious that the puppy was hungry.

ourselves.d: to refer to 'me and other people like me'
ourselves.e: We cleaned the house by ourselves.

opposite.e: Hot and cold are opposite temperatures.

object.d: A thing that can be seen or touched
object.e: My favorite object on my desk is the snow globe.

offering.d: Something that is given or provided
offering.e: The bake sale was an offering to raise money for the school.

opportunities.e: The student had the opportunity to be class president

organized.d: When things are put in a neat and tidy way
organized.e: My desk looks so organized after I cleaned it up

obtained.e: The student obtained a high score on the test.

occur.e: A solar eclipse can occur once a year.

orange.d: A bright, round fruit that is orange in color
orange.e: I like to eat oranges for a healthy snack.

opposed.e: The students were opposed to the new school rules.

objects.d: Things that you can see and touch.
objects.e: There are many different objects on my desk.

owners.d: People who have control or possession of something.
owners.e: The owners of the pet store are very friendly.

officially.d: In a way that is formal or approved.
officially.e: The president officially opened the new school.

observed.e: The teacher observed the students working on their project.

obtain.e: She was able to obtain tickets to the concert.

offices.e: My mom works in big offices downtown.

operate.d: To make something work or run
operate.e: The teacher operates the computer to show us a video.

organisation.d: a group of people who work together for a common purpose
organisation.e: The charity organization helps people in need.
